business_ideas.idea: Local news franchise business - local reporting in 50K-300K person cities
Copywriting service/course - teaching email/newsletter copy techniques
Newsletter business - personalized email pitching businesses

predictions.text: Beware 10 out of 10 pitches - great pitches often hide mediocre businesses
Local news business will scale to multiple cities profitably
Copywriting ability is a hidden superpower for business success

disagreements: Andrew pushes back against dogmatic no-raise bootstrap philosophy of Basecamp/37signals. Says nuance matters - sometimes raising money is logical. Sam/Shaan mostly listen and learn rather than directly clash.
dynamics: Andrew drives the episode as the experienced operator with stories and frameworks. Sam and Shaan are notably quiet this episode (connection issues, listening mode). Andrew teaches through examples. Tone is mentorship/learning rather than debate. Energy builds through copywriting discussion.
frameworks: Hire 1 to hire 10: first employee should be able to grow to managing 10 people
Copywriting AIDA formula: Attention, Interest, Desire, Action
Hook with first line: most important sentence of anything you write
Pick fights while taking moral high ground: criticize systems, not people
Beware the 10 out of 10 charm pitch: if something can't go wrong, it's terrifying
Don't ask how great people do great things: ask how they think about them
learnings: First sentence must hook; second-rate pitches mask great opportunities and vice versa
Good copywriting pulls emotional hooks (shock, outrage) then provides context and moat
Pitching via written form (email, Medium) is underutilized compared to in-person pitches
Storytelling can be so compelling it overrides proof - requires intellectual honesty about nuance
Local markets ignored by national companies become defensible moats

banger_quotes.quote: Beware of the 10 out of 10 pitch with perfect charm
The first sentence is the most important sentence of anything you write
The only reason MetaLab worked was picking fights about boring topics
I got hacked good - sat in bath, got text phishing attack, gave them my Twitter 2FA code

numbers_dropped: Andrew got hacked; scammers collected ~$2,500 from fake PlayStation/St. Jude giveaway
Andrew's news business just hit profitability in Victoria (first market)
Zero-day exploits cost $1-3 million to acquire
notable: Guest interview with Andrew Wilkinson on operational growth; 'hire 1 to hire 10' framework is explicitly called out as valuable insight by both hosts; cybersecurity hacking story adds dramatic tension; copywriting discussion becomes deep technical masterclass
